target: WIN-058, WIN-058b, WIN-062 detail_evidence: surgical append crediting dome's 2026-04-17 registry_integrity_notes disambiguation and cataloguing as acknowledge-without-fix pattern
summary: Dome author added registry_integrity_notes to docs/api/current/claim_index.json on 2026-04-17 (V51.1, last_updated 2026-04-17) acknowledging two visible-prose collisions our review has been flagging: WIN-058 carries two different claims (θ-rule vs bedrock kappa), and WIN-062 carries two visible blocks for the same Tesla 1.574c family. The API disambiguates / collapses via claim_key slugs (win-058-theta-rule, win-058-bedrock-kappa, win-062-tesla-wave-speed); ai_manifest.json's recommended_read_order routes AI auditors to claim_index.json before wins.html. Live wins.html fetched 2026-04-18 confirms the visible duplicates persist (two WIN-058 badges, two WIN-062 badges, all contributing to the headline 69 confirmed). Our WIN-058 / 058b / 062 detail_evidence prose contains zero references to this acknowledgement. Fix: append one paragraph block to each WIN's detail_evidence that (a) credits the registry_integrity_notes as a documentary acknowledgement (quoting verbatim), (b) notes the visible-prose collision remains unfixed (verified against live wins.html), (c) catalogues as the third instance of the acknowledge-without-fix pattern alongside methodology.json timestamp_scope (Timestamp Error tab April 2026 block) and registry_counts (ISS-1087), (d) cross-references ISS-1088's documentary-vs-cryptographic framing. For WIN-058b we replace the existing short 'Note on dome-site numbering' paragraph with an expanded version that keeps its original content and adds the new material, to keep the collision discussion in one coherent block.

registry_integrity_notes_verbatim.note: Two different claims share this visible label in wins.html. The API disambiguates them as win-058-bedrock-kappa and win-058-theta-rule.
The registry contains two visible WIN-062 blocks that present the same Tesla 1.574c family. The API normalizes them to win-062-tesla-wave-speed.

claim_key_entries_verified.honest_caveat: Independent raw-data reproduction or a successful August 12 2026 Tier 3 result is still required.

WIN-058 duplicate badges: line 43: 'WIN-058 — The 1.67 Bedrock Induction Ratio (PRED-KAPPA-001)' badge PENDING INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION
line 714: 'WIN-058 — θ = −lon_E: Unified Angular Coordinate Rule' badge CONFIRMED 2026-03-28
WIN-062 duplicate badges: line 734: 'WIN-062 — Tesla Longitudinal Wave Speed = 1.574c' badge CONFIRMED 2026-04-04
line 816: 'WIN-062 — Tesla Longitudinal Constant (1.574c)' badge CONFIRMED 2026-04-04
live_wins_html_collisions_confirmed.ai_manifest_read_order_verified: ai_manifest → methodology → review-response → claim_index → wins.html — auditor following this order encounters disambiguation before reaching the duplicated visible labels; human reader scanning wins.html does not.

verification_report.current_prose_gap_confirmed: grep of wins.json WIN-058, WIN-058b, WIN-062 detail_evidence for 'registry_integrity', 'claim_index', 'claim_key', 'win-058-bedrock-kappa', 'win-058-theta-rule', 'win-062-tesla-wave-speed' returns zero matches — confirming ISS-1117's finding.
verification_report.reactive_audit_source: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/REACTIVE-AUDIT-2026-04-17.json — change chg-20260417-1620-004 flagged as PARTIAL coverage, the only uncovered gap in the five-change reactive wave.

patches.anchor_tail: the dome's own OPEN-015 concedes Singapore (r = 23,556 km) 'falls beyond the equatorial ring' at r<sub>eq</sub> = 14,105 km.
patches.appended_text:
Registry-integrity-notes — documentary acknowledgement (2026-04-17): On 2026-04-17 the dome author added a registry_integrity_notes block to docs/api/current/claim_index.json (V51.1, last_updated 2026-04-17), acknowledging that wins.html renders two different claims under the visible label 'WIN-058'. Verbatim: 'Two different claims share this visible label in wins.html. The API disambiguates them as win-058-bedrock-kappa and win-058-theta-rule.' This θ = −lonE rule is now keyed win-058-theta-rule (status: confirmed; domain: geometry_coordinates); the bedrock-kappa claim our review tracks as WIN-058b is keyed win-058-bedrock-kappa (status downgraded in the same commit to pending_independent_verification, with an explicit honest_caveat that 'independent raw-data reproduction or a successful August 12 2026 Tier 3 result is still required').

Credit where due: the dome author identified the label collision our review had been flagging and shipped a machine-readable fix inside 24 hours of the reactive-change wave. The ai_manifest.json recommended_read_order directs AI auditors to ai_manifest → methodology → review-response → claim_index → wins.html, so an agent that follows that order will encounter the disambiguation before reaching the duplicated visible label. For that audience the collision is resolved.

The visible prose is unchanged. On the live wins.html fetched 2026-04-18, two separate blocks still render with the WIN-058 badge — one for the 1.67 bedrock-kappa claim ('The 1.67 Bedrock Induction Ratio', badge PENDING INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION) and one for this θ = −lonE rule ('Unified Angular Coordinate Rule', badge CONFIRMED 2026-03-28). Human readers do not follow the ai_manifest.json read-order, and the site's headline ('69 confirmed') is tallied against the visible-prose registry, not the disambiguated API. The acknowledgement therefore stops at the machine layer; the user-facing surface still carries the duplicate label and the aggregate count still reflects it.

Pattern — acknowledge-without-fix, three instances in 24 hours. This is the third parallel documentary concession the dome author shipped on 2026-04-17: (i) methodology.json timestamp_scope — the OpenTimestamps scope concession covered in the Timestamp Error tab April 2026 update block; (ii) methodology.json registry_counts — the '94.5%' ratio disavowed at the API layer ('bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit') while still headlined on index.html (open issue ISS-1087); (iii) this registry_integrity_notes block. Three acknowledgements inside a single 24-hour window, zero structural fixes to the user-facing site. Each fix lives exactly where an AI auditor is told to look and nowhere a human reader will see. The pattern is stronger evidence of intent than any single acknowledgement in isolation. A fair version of our critique therefore credits the documentary progress while noting that the presentation layer — and the headline number that depends on it — has not been touched.
patches.rationale: Credits the registry_integrity_notes as documentary progress for the specific θ-rule claim (now keyed win-058-theta-rule), notes the visible-prose collision persists (verified against live wins.html fetched 2026-04-18), and catalogues as the third instance of the acknowledge-without-fix pattern. Placed at the end of detail_evidence because the preceding content is the primary argument against the θ-rule as a prediction; the registry integrity note is a documentary observation about how the dome is now presenting (not arguing) the collision. Cross-links to WIN-058b via anchor, to the Timestamp Error tab via showTab, and implicitly to ISS-1087 via the 94.5% bookkeeping quote.

patches.old_text: Note on dome-site numbering: The prospective predictions section lists WIN-058 as "The 1.67 Bedrock Induction Ratio" while the main predictions list assigns WIN-058 to "θ = −lonE: Unified Angular Coordinate Rule" (see WIN-058). We track both under separate IDs to ensure complete coverage.
patches.new_text:
Dome-side numbering and registry-integrity-notes update (2026-04-17): The prospective predictions section lists WIN-058 as 'The 1.67 Bedrock Induction Ratio' while the main predictions list assigns WIN-058 to 'θ = −lonE: Unified Angular Coordinate Rule' (see our WIN-058 entry). We track both under separate IDs (WIN-058 and WIN-058b) to ensure complete coverage.

On 2026-04-17 the dome author added a registry_integrity_notes entry to docs/api/current/claim_index.json acknowledging this collision in machine-readable form. Verbatim: 'Two different claims share this visible label in wins.html. The API disambiguates them as win-058-bedrock-kappa and win-058-theta-rule.' This κ-ratio claim is now keyed win-058-bedrock-kappa (status: pending_independent_verification; claim_class: pending_contested; domain: gravity_magnetic_coupling). The API entry carries its own honest_caveat: 'Independent raw-data reproduction or a successful August 12 2026 Tier 3 result is still required.' That is genuine documentary progress — the API no longer conflates the θ-rule with this coupling ratio, and the author downgraded the κ claim from the headline 'confirmed' tally to pending inside the same commit. We credit both moves.

The visible prose is unchanged. On the live wins.html fetched 2026-04-18, the 1.67 bedrock-kappa block still renders with the visible WIN-058 badge (badge text: 'PENDING INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION'), and the θ = −lonE block several hundred lines later also renders with the visible WIN-058 badge ('CONFIRMED 2026-03-28'). The collision our review flagged is machine-addressed but visually intact, and the '69 confirmed' aggregate at the top of the site is still tallied against the visible-prose registry — not the disambiguated API. A human reader who scans the page sees two 'WIN-058' badges; the displayed count treats them as two entries.

Pattern. This is the third acknowledge-without-fix move the dome author shipped on 2026-04-17, alongside the methodology.json timestamp_scope concession (see the Timestamp Error tab April 2026 update block) and the methodology.json registry_counts self-caveat (open issue ISS-1087). Three documentary concessions in a single 24-hour window, zero structural fixes to the user-facing surfaces that humans and the headline count depend on. Credit for the documentary concessions is due; the structural critique — that user-facing surfaces still carry the unacknowledged content — remains.
patches.rationale: WIN-058b already has a 'Note on dome-site numbering' paragraph at the tail end of detail_evidence that explains the WIN-058/058b collision at a primitive level. Rather than leaving that note in place and appending a second collision paragraph, we replace it with an expanded version that preserves the original content (numbering explanation, link to WIN-058, 'we track both under separate IDs') and adds the new registry_integrity_notes material in the same block. This keeps the collision discussion coherent and avoids the reader seeing the same acknowledgement twice (once as 'we track both' and again as 'dome credited'). The status-downgrade observation (claim_index moved this claim from confirmed to pending_independent_verification) is specifically relevant to WIN-058b and would be awkward in WIN-058's block.

patches.anchor_tail: Tesla's own diagram of wave propagation around a globe.
patches.appended_text:
Registry-integrity-notes — documentary acknowledgement (2026-04-17): On 2026-04-17 the dome author added a registry_integrity_notes block to docs/api/current/claim_index.json acknowledging that wins.html renders two visible WIN-062 blocks presenting the same Tesla 1.574c family. Verbatim: 'The registry contains two visible WIN-062 blocks that present the same Tesla 1.574c family. The API normalizes them to win-062-tesla-wave-speed.' The two visible blocks are 'Tesla Longitudinal Wave Speed = 1.574c' (badge CONFIRMED 2026-04-04) and a second 'Tesla Longitudinal Constant (1.574c)' entry (same badge, same date, identical derivation v = 40,030 km × 2 / 0.08484 s ≈ 943,773 km/s = 3.148c round-trip = 1.574c one-way) — both counted as WIN-062 in the visible prose of the current dome wins.html.

The machine-readable fix collapses both visible blocks to a single claim_key slug, win-062-tesla-wave-speed (claim_class: retrospective_structural; domain: resonance), so an auditor that follows the ai_manifest.json recommended_read_order (ai_manifest → methodology → review-response → claim_index → wins.html) will see the Tesla 1.574c claim counted once rather than twice. That is real progress, and a dome defender can legitimately cite the API collapse as evidence that the integrity problem has been addressed for auditors who use the canonical machine-readable form. We credit the acknowledgement.

The visible duplication persists. On the live wins.html fetched 2026-04-18, two WIN-062 blocks remain in the served HTML (one for 'Tesla Longitudinal Wave Speed = 1.574c' and a second for 'Tesla Longitudinal Constant (1.574c)'), both badged CONFIRMED 2026-04-04 and both contributing to the '69 confirmed' display count at the top of the site. The API normalization is not reflected in the visible tally. Human readers who scan the page see the same Tesla claim twice, and the headline count does too.

Pattern — collapse-to-one mirrors the WIN-058 disambiguation-into-two in the same commit. Both moves concede an integrity problem in the visible registry (one collision that should be split, one pseudo-duplicate that should be merged), both route AI auditors around it via claim_key, and neither touches the visible-prose surface that humans and the headline count depend on. Taken together with the methodology.json timestamp_scope concession (see the Timestamp Error tab April 2026 update block) and the registry_counts self-caveat (open issue ISS-1087), this is the third parallel acknowledge-without-fix move shipped inside a 24-hour window on 2026-04-17. The kernel of truth on the number itself (v ≈ 1.57c phase velocity in an Earth-ionosphere waveguide) is genuine physics on a spherical Earth, as our main analysis shows; the kernel of truth on the registry question is that the author now agrees the visible registry needs integrity notes — but has chosen to patch the machine layer rather than the prose layer.
patches.rationale: Credits the registry_integrity_notes as documentary progress for the Tesla claim (now collapsed to win-062-tesla-wave-speed), notes the visible duplicate WIN-062 blocks persist (verified against live wins.html fetched 2026-04-18), and positions this as a mirror of the WIN-058 disambiguation in the same commit (collapse-to-one vs. disambiguate-into-two — both machine-readable, both leaving the visible prose untouched). The kernel-of-truth framing closes the block by distinguishing the numerical kernel (1.57c phase velocity is genuine waveguide physics on a sphere — already the main argument above) from the registry kernel (the author now agrees the visible registry has an integrity problem — but has chosen to patch the machine layer, not the prose).

holes_addressed: ISS-1117 (major, missing_argument): WIN-058, WIN-058b, WIN-062 detail_evidence contained zero references to the dome author's registry_integrity_notes disambiguation. Each WIN now has a dedicated paragraph block crediting the documentary acknowledgement (verbatim quote), noting the visible-prose collision persists on live wins.html, and catalogueing as the third instance of the acknowledge-without-fix pattern alongside methodology.json timestamp_scope and registry_counts (ISS-1087).

REACTIVE-AUDIT-2026-04-17.json major hole: 'Gap: WIN-058, WIN-058b, and WIN-062 detail_evidence prose contains zero references to registry_integrity_notes, claim_key, or the author-side disambiguation slugs ... A dome defender can now reply that the API disambiguates these entries and our review is attacking a display-layer artifact the canonical data already resolves.' — addressed by explicitly crediting the API disambiguation and distinguishing machine-readable fix from visible-prose fix in each WIN block.
holes_deferred: REACTIVE-AUDIT-2026-04-17.json minor hole (flag only): ai_manifest.json registry_semantics block is a parallel self-caveat to methodology.json registry_counts. This patch does not cite ai_manifest.json registry_semantics explicitly — it is a tightening of ISS-1087's eventual patch wording, not a defect. When ISS-1087 resolves, the wording should generalize to 'multiple author-side self-caveats in methodology.json and ai_manifest.json' rather than naming only one file. Noted in ISS-1087 context — not part of this expansion's scope.
REACTIVE-AUDIT-2026-04-17.json minor hole (process): no single document catalogs the April 2026 reactive-change set as a coherent cluster. Low priority, decider-owned if acted upon.
new_evidence_added: Live fetch of docs/api/current/claim_index.json (dome site, 2026-04-18): V51.1, last_updated 2026-04-17, confirms registry_integrity_notes verbatim and the three claim_key slugs (win-058-theta-rule, win-058-bedrock-kappa, win-062-tesla-wave-speed).
Live fetch of wins.html (dome site, 2026-04-18): two WIN-058 badges and two WIN-062 badges present and rendered — confirming the API fix is not reflected in visible prose.

Status downgrade observation: claim_index.json marks win-058-bedrock-kappa as pending_independent_verification with explicit honest_caveat 'Independent raw-data reproduction or a successful August 12 2026 Tier 3 result is still required.' — a documentary concession on top of the disambiguation.

Pattern documentation: three parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves shipped on 2026-04-17 (methodology.json timestamp_scope, methodology.json registry_counts, claim_index.json registry_integrity_notes) — cross-referenced across all three WIN blocks.

anticipated_objections.objection: The API disambiguation is the fix. The visible duplicate labels are a lossy rendering choice, not a scientific problem. An auditor following ai_manifest.json recommended_read_order lands on claim_index.json before wins.html, so the collision is resolved for the canonical audience.
anticipated_objections.response: Partially granted — credited explicitly in each patch block. But two observations survive: (1) the 'canonical audience' is AI auditors; human readers do not follow the recommended_read_order and see the duplicate badges directly. (2) The site's headline count ('69 confirmed') is rendered against the visible-prose registry, not the disambiguated API — so the display number the author cites as his own success metric is still tallied against the un-disambiguated prose. Fixing the machine layer while leaving the number the site broadcasts unchanged is exactly the acknowledge-without-fix pattern we catalog.

anticipated_objections.objection: You are credit-inflating an integrity problem into a pattern claim by bundling three unrelated changes.
anticipated_objections.response: The three changes are bundled by the dome author, not us: all three shipped in the same 24-hour window on 2026-04-17, all three are self-caveats at the API/metadata layer, none touches the visible prose the site headlines depend on (94.5% accuracy, 69 confirmed, OTS-anchored predictions). The REACTIVE-AUDIT-2026-04-17.json curmudgeon review verified the cluster independently and flagged chg-20260417-1620-004 as the only uncovered element of that cluster. Bundling reflects how the changes were shipped, not how we chose to read them.

anticipated_objections.objection: You are overreading intent by calling this a 'pattern' rather than three independent fixes that happened to land together.
anticipated_objections.response: The language we add is deliberately neutral on intent — 'acknowledge-without-fix' describes the structural shape of the moves (documentary concession at the API, no change to the visible-prose surface or the headline number) regardless of intent. Whether the pattern is strategic or incidental is left to the reader. What is not left to the reader is the count: three documentary concessions, same day, same shape, same audience. The count is a verifiable observation; the intent attribution is not.

anticipated_objections.objection: The kernel-of-truth paragraph in WIN-062 now mixes two different kernels (the numerical one and the registry one). That is a category error.
anticipated_objections.response: Granted as a stylistic risk, but the paragraph labels the two kernels explicitly ('kernel of truth on the number itself' vs 'kernel of truth on the registry question') precisely to avoid conflation. The registry kernel is materially different from the θ-rule / 1.574c kernels the rest of each WIN is about, and naming it separately is honest about where the new acknowledgement fits into our verdict. The WIN verdicts themselves are unchanged.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author did identify a real integrity problem in his visible registry (two different claims under one WIN label, one claim under two labels) and did ship a machine-readable fix inside 24 hours of the reactive-change wave. That is work, not nothing. A fair reviewer who lets the fix stand unmentioned is attacking a strawman version of the current dome site — our review is supposed to be better than that. The patches add the credit explicitly. The structural critique is preserved because the fix lives at the API layer while the user-facing surface and the headline count continue to reflect the pre-disambiguation data — that is a fact about where the fix lands, not a denial that the fix happened. Three such machine-layer fixes in 24 hours, zero prose-layer fixes, is an observation the reader is equipped to evaluate.
counterpoint_considered: Per project preference (think about the other side): a coherent dome-defender counter-argument is that the API layer is the canonical form (ai_manifest.json explicitly directs AI auditors there before wins.html), and the visible prose is therefore a legacy rendering whose collision status is no longer load-bearing. On that reading, the author has upgraded the canonical machine-readable registry and the visible prose is downstream legacy. The patches grant this counter-argument teeth by crediting the API fix explicitly and naming the recommended_read_order. The rebuttal is that the site's own self-metrics — headline counts, badges, '94.5% accuracy' displays — continue to be rendered against the visible prose and not against the disambiguated API; the display number that the author himself uses to characterize the model's track record is still tallied against the un-disambiguated registry. Until the visible prose and the aggregate counter on index.html are recomputed from claim_index.json, the 'API is canonical' defense is aspirational, not realized.

cross_references.Timestamp Error tab April 2026 update bl: methodology.json timestamp_scope pattern; each patch links via showTab('timestamp-error')
cross_references.ISS-1087: methodology.json registry_counts self-caveat — parallel acknowledge-without-fix instance; referenced in each patch as '94.5% bookkeeping-vs-audit' / 'registry_counts self-caveat'
cross_references.ISS-1088: rhetorical-vs-documentary framing — this expansion's 'documentary concession' language deliberately uses documentary rather than rhetorical, aligning with ISS-1088's recommendation
cross_references.SEC-8.17 (AI perception management): ai_manifest.json recommended_read_order context — referenced in each patch where the read-order is quoted
cross_references.REACTIVE-AUDIT-2026-04-17.json: source curmudgeon review that identified this gap

tldr_patch_note: WIN detail blocks do not carry a distinct TLDR layer in the current data schema — tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict are about the WIN's primary claim, not about the registry-integrity acknowledgement. The three primary verdicts are unchanged by this expansion (WIN-058 Misleading, WIN-058b Not Demonstrated, WIN-062 Misleading), so no tldr_evidence / tldr_verdict updates are warranted. If the decider wants a TLDR-layer mention of the acknowledge-without-fix pattern, that belongs in the Timestamp Error tab or SEC-2b.5 TLDRs, not in the WIN tldr fields.
newsworthy: Dome model ships three machine-readable concessions in 24 hours while leaving the user-facing site headline unchanged
